session.assistant:
I see that `test_yaml_block_round_trip` uses `get_yaml_block_types()` to parametrize. This returns all YAML block types including `"erk-plan"` (backward-compat alias) and `"erk-pr"`.
The `SAMPLE_DATA` dict now has `"erk-pr"` but NOT `"erk-plan"`. The backward-compat `"erk-plan"` registry entry will cause `test_yaml_block_round_trip` to try to look up `SAMPLE_DATA["erk-plan"]` which doesn't exist.
I need to add `"erk-plan"` back to `SAMPLE_DATA` for backward-compat, or... let me re-read the test:
```python @pytest.mark.parametrize("key", [info.key for info in get_yaml_block_types()], ids=str) def test_yaml_block_round_trip(self, key: str) -> None: data = SAMPLE_DATA[key] ```
`get_yaml_block_types()` returns items with `category == YAML`. The backward-compat entry has `key="erk-plan"`. So `SAMPLE_DATA["erk-plan"]` would be missing.
I need to add `"erk-plan"` to `SAMPLE_DATA` as well, OR I need to skip it in the test, OR I should also add `"erk-plan"` to `FACTORY_BLOCKS`.

session.assistant:
Wait, but the round-trip test for `"erk-plan"` will try to render it with key `"erk-plan"` and parse it back. When rendered with key `"erk-plan"`, it produces `<!-- erk:metadata-block:erk-plan -->`. When parsed, it should find the registry entry for `"erk-plan"` and return a `MetadataBlock(key="erk-plan", data=...)`.
But the test then asserts `result.blocks[0].key == key` where key is `"erk-plan"`. This should work since the registry entry has `key="erk-plan"`.
However, there's a subtle issue: the `TestSchemaBackedRoundTrip` test that uses `FACTORY_BLOCKS` might also fail if it tries to check `"erk-plan"` block:
session.tool_result:
295→ """Factory -> render -> parse -> schema.validate() for schema-backed types.""" 296→ 297→ @pytest.mark.parametrize("key", SCHEMA_BACKED_KEYS, ids=str) 298→ def test_schema_factory_round_trip(self, key: str) -> None: 299→ block = FACTORY_BLOCKS[key] 300→ rendered = render_metadata_block(block) 301→ result = parse_metadata_blocks(rendered) 302→ 303→ assert not result.has_errors, f"Parse errors for {key}: {result.errors}" 304→ assert len(result.blocks) == 1 305→ 306→ info = get_block_type(key) 307→ assert info is not None, f"Block type {key} not found in registry" 308→ assert info.schema is not None, f"Block type {key} should have a schema" 309→ # Schema validation should not raise 310→ info.schema.validate(result.blocks[0].data) 311→ 312→ 313→class TestRegistryCompleteness: 314→ """Every registered block type has sample data for round-trip testing.""" 315→ 316→ def test_all_registered_types_have_sample_data(self) -> None: 317→ all_keys = set(get_all_block_types().keys()) 318→ yaml_keys = set(SAMPLE_DATA.keys()) 319→ content_keys = set(SAMPLE_CONTENT.keys()) 320→ covered_keys = yaml_keys | content_keys 321→ 322→ missing = all_keys - covered_keys 323→ assert not missing, f"Block types missing sample data: {missing}" 324→ assert covered_keys == all_keys
... [30 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
There are completeness tests that check: 1. `all_registered_types_have_sample_data` - all registry keys must be in SAMPLE_DATA or SAMPLE_CONTENT. 2. `yaml_sample_data_matches_yaml_registry` - `SAMPLE_DATA.keys() == yaml_registry_keys` 3. `factory_blocks_cover_all_schema_backed_types` - `FACTORY_BLOCKS.keys() == schema_backed_keys` 4. `test_registry_has_expected_count` - 16 total, 13 yaml, 3 content
Currently I added `"erk-plan"` to both SAMPLE_DATA and FACTORY_BLOCKS. With the backward-compat alias, the registry now has 17 entries instead of 16 (added `"erk-plan"` entry). But the test expects exactly 16.
This is getting complex. Let me think about the right approach:
**The backward-compat alias `"erk-plan"` in the registry is intentional** - existing GitHub PRs have `<!-- erk:metadata-block:erk-plan -->` markers and need to be parseable.
The round-trip test needs to be updated to handle the new count. But I should update it carefully. Let me count what the registry has after my changes:
Before my changes: - YAML with schemas: plan-header, objective-header, erk-plan, erk-worktree-creation, erk-implementation-status, workflow-started, submission-queued, plan-retry (8) - YAML without schemas: impl-started, impl-ended, learn-invoked, tripwire-candidates, objective-roadmap (5) - CONTENT: plan-body, objective-body, planning-session-prompts (3) - Total: 16
After my changes (added `erk-pr` as new canonical + kept `erk-plan` as backward-compat alias): - YAML with schemas: plan-header, objective-header, **erk-pr**, **erk-plan (alias)**, erk-worktree-creation, erk-implementation-status, workflow-started, submission-queued, plan-retry (9) - YAML without schemas: impl-started, impl-ended, learn-invoked, tripwire-candidates, objective-roadmap (5) - CONTENT: plan-body, objective-body, planning-session-prompts (3) - Total: 17
So I need to update the count assertion in the test. Let me update it:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: assert len(all_types) == 16 assert len(yaml_types) == 13 assert len(content_types) == 3
assert len(all_types) == 17 assert len(yaml_types) == 14 assert len(content_types) == 3

1. `"erk-planning"` in `codex_portable.py` and `bundled.py` - skill name (NOT changing per plan) 2. `"erk-plan"` backward-compat alias in `log_cmd.py` - intentional 3. `"erk-plan"` in `test_plan_file_operations.py` - a filename reference (not a label/key, fine to keep) 4. `"erk-plan"` in `test_round_trip.py` - backward-compat test data (intentional) 5. `"erk-plan"` in `registry.py` - backward-compat alias (intentional) 6. `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` - env var name (different concept, NOT changing per plan)
